# go-oauth-client-authn

> Composable net/http round-trippers for OAuth 2.0 client authentication.

go-oauth-client-authn implements the OAuth 2.0 client-authentication methods as
small, orthogonal http.RoundTripper decorators. Pick a method, wrap a base
transport, and every request the transport carries to a token endpoint is
authenticated — with no coupling to any particular OAuth client library. It is
framework-neutral and depends only on the standard library plus go-jose/v4 (for
the JWT assertion methods). Requires Go 1.26 or newer.

Module path: github.com/hstern/go-oauth-client-authn (package authn)

## Composition core

- `Method` — interface for a single client-authentication method; decorates a
  base http.RoundTripper.
- `Transport(m Method, base http.RoundTripper) http.RoundTripper` — applies m,
  defaulting base to http.DefaultTransport.
- `NewClient(m Method, base http.RoundTripper) *http.Client` — wraps the
  result in a ready *http.Client to hand to a token-family client.

The wire effect is derived per request, so one configured method authenticates
token, pushed-authorization, introspection, and revocation requests alike.

## Methods

- `client_secret_basic` (RFC 6749 §2.3.1) — `ClientSecretBasic(clientID, clientSecret string) Method`
- `client_secret_post` (RFC 6749 §2.3.1) — `ClientSecretPost(clientID, clientSecret string) Method`
- `client_secret_jwt` (RFC 7523, HS256) — `ClientSecretJWT(clientID, clientSecret string, opts ...AssertionOption) Method`
- `private_key_jwt` (RFC 7523, RS/ES/PS) — `PrivateKeyJWT(clientID string, signer crypto.Signer, opts ...AssertionOption) Method`
- `tls_client_auth` (RFC 8705) — `TLSClientAuth(clientID string, cert tls.Certificate, opts ...TLSOption) Method`
- `self_signed_tls_client_auth` (RFC 8705) — `SelfSignedTLSClientAuth(clientID string, cert tls.Certificate, opts ...TLSOption) Method`

## Options

- Assertion (RFC 7523 methods): `WithAudience`, `WithAssertionLifetime`,
  `WithKeyID`, `WithRSAPSS`. With no audience set, the assertion `aud` is
  derived from the request URL.
- TLS (RFC 8705 methods): `WithServerName`.

## Key input

- `SignerFromPEM(pemBytes []byte) (crypto.Signer, error)` — PEM block to signer.
- `SignerFromJWK(jwkJSON []byte) (crypto.Signer, error)` — JWK to signer.

Both produce a crypto.Signer for PrivateKeyJWT.

## Usage

    method := authn.ClientSecretBasic("client-id", "client-secret")
    client := authn.NewClient(method, nil)
    // client.Do(req) now authenticates every token-endpoint request.

## Links

- README: https://github.com/hstern/go-oauth-client-authn/blob/main/README.md
- API reference: https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/hstern/go-oauth-client-authn
